content_blocks.text:
numbers and statics
you are here
277
Static methods can’t use non-static 
(instance) variables!
Static methods run without knowing about any particular 
instance of the static method’5 class. And as you saw on the 
previous pages, there might not even be any instances of that 
class. Since a static method is called using the class (Math.
random()) as opposed to an instance reference (t2.play()), 
a static method can’t refer to any instance variables of the 
class. The static method doesn’t know which instance’5 
variable value to use.
If you try to use an 
instance variable from 
inside a static method, 
the compiler thinks, 
“1 don’t know which 
object’5 instance variable 
you’re talking about!” 
If you have ten Duck 
objects on the heap, a 
static method doesn’t 
know about any of them.
public class Duck {
 private int size;
 public static void main (String[] args) {
 System.out.println(“Size of duck is “ + size);
 }
 public void setSize(int 5) {
 size = 5;
 }
 public int getSize() {
 return size;
 }
}
 
If you try to compile this code:
1’m sure they’re 
talking about MY 
size variable.
No, 1’m pretty sure 
they’re talking about 
MY size variable.
Which Duck? 
Whose size?
You’11 get this error:
File Edit Window Help Quack
% javac Duck.java
Duck.java:6: non-static variable 
size cannot be referenced from a 
static context
 System.out.println(“Size 
of duck is “ + size);
 ^
If there’5 a Duck on 
the heap somewhere, we 
don’t know about it.

rows:
% javac Duck.java
Duck.java:6: non-static variable
